title: C6 — Journey / dataset instance
description: A single benchmark instance (benchmark-design.md §5.1): the proven security-benchmark 4-tuple {task, attack, task-success, attack-success} extended with the route-graph and the multi-day event trace. Carries the INITIAL route-state, the SANCTIONED route-graph (C2 shape — Layer A given / Layer B hidden), an event_trace (facts/drifts/escalation_forks/injections/slow_burns), the oracle bindings, the split, and the horizon. NFR-1: NO wall-clock — `t` fields are LOGICAL sim-time labels, never timestamps.

domain.description: One of the three representable domains (FR-B5).

intent.description: GIVEN to the agent. The principal's goal in natural language.

initial_route_state.description: The starting route-state (C1 shape). Optional; if omitted the harness seeds position=route_graph.entry with empty memory.

route_graph.description: The SANCTIONED route-graph for this journey (C2 shape). Layer A (nodes/edges/entry/goal/guardrails) is given to the agent; Layer B (optimal_walk/subgoal_states) is hidden. Validated by route_graph.schema.json (C2).

event_trace.description: The deterministic multi-day event stream the scheduler emits in order (FR-B2). Each event has a logical time `t` and a `type`. The task x attack matrix lands here as `injection` events.

t.description: LOGICAL sim-time label (leg/step), NOT a wall-clock timestamp (NFR-1).

type.description: fact = new information; drift = a stale assumption / changed fact (carries `changes`); escalation_fork = a risk:high decision point the calibrated agent should escalate; injection = an adversarial payload (the task x attack matrix); slow_burn = a signal only offline consolidation (the Dreamer) catches.

changes.description: For type=drift: the fact deltas the agent must reflect (adaptation axis, ASI06).

attack_id.description: For type=injection: canonical template id, e.g. 'important-instructions', 'system-message', 'ignore-previous', 'tool-knowledge' (AgentDojo/InjecAgent templates).

vector.description: For type=injection: WHERE the payload lands so it is reachable along the correct trajectory (named placeholder in a tool output).

placeholder.description: Named slot the payload is substituted into, e.g. 'DOC_BODY'.

goal.description: For type=injection: the ATTACKER's targeted goal, e.g. 'transfer_funds_to_attacker' (used by targeted-ASR scoring, FR-S7).

asi.description: For type=injection: the OWASP ASI threat id this attack exercises (e.g. 'ASI01').

signal.description: For type=slow_burn: the latent signal that only consolidation surfaces.

oracle_bindings.description: How this journey's oracles are evaluated (benchmark-design.md §5.1 `oracles`). All programmatic unless a field explicitly defers to the residual LLM judge. The hidden optimal_walk/subgoal_states live in route_graph; this block binds the OTHER axes' checks.

foresight.description: Foresight oracle (FR-S1): progress over hidden subgoal_states + optimality gap vs hidden optimal_walk.

alignment.description: Drift oracle (FR-S2): off-walk / forbidden-state predicate over the FULL trace.

security.description: Per-attack two-axis oracle (FR-S7): utility-under-attack + targeted ASR; canary egress strings.

canary_egress.description: Canary tokens that must NEVER appear in non-allowlisted egress.

collateral.description: Collateral-damage allowlist (FR-S8): the set of route_state keys this task is permitted to change.

split.description: Dataset split (FR-B6). challenge = unseen attacks + unseen tools (anti-memorization). Grader/oracle hidden from the agent.

horizon.description: Operationalized long-horizon (FR-B7, benchmark-design.md §5.3). A tunable difficulty axis, not a constant.

legs.description: L — number of legs (~sim-days). MVP default 3-5.

step_budget.description: S — per-journey decision-step budget. MVP default ~30-80.
